name: Komi
notes: Komi (коми кыв), a Permic (Uralic) language of the Komi Republic, Russia, covering Komi-Zyrian and Komi-Permyak. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Cyrillic alphabet — the Russian alphabet plus ⟨І і⟩ and ⟨Ӧ ӧ⟩ (/ɘ/). A full spec must model the palatalisation contrast (soft sign, iotated vowel letters, and the hard-⟨і⟩ vs ⟨и⟩ distinction that marks the preceding consonant's hardness), the affricates written ⟨дж дз тш⟩ (multigraphs, ambiguous with letter sequences), and the Russian-loanword letters. Komi was historically written in the Old Permic (Abur) and Molodtsov alphabets; input is assumed to be the modern Cyrillic orthography.
